quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 7 Quiz 4
quiz.subtitle: 7.4: Solving Exponential Equations Using Logarithms
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Solving for Exponents using Logs and Lns

questions.question: Solve $4^x = 20$. Round to three decimal places.
Solve $5e^{2x} = 35$. Give the exact answer and round to three decimal places.
questions.explanation: Isolate the exponential: divide by 5 тЖТ $e^{2x} = 7$. Take ln: $2x = \ln 7$. $x = \frac{\ln 7}{2} \approx \frac{1.9459}{2} \approx 0.973$. Always isolate $e^{kx}$ before taking ln.

questions.question: Solve $1.5^x = 8$. Round to two decimal places. Given $\log 1.5 \approx 0.1761$ and $\log 8 \approx 0.9031$.
Solve $3^{x} + 3^{x} = 54$.
questions.explanation: Combine like terms: $2 \cdot 3^x = 54$ тЖТ $3^x = 27 = 3^3$ тЖТ $x = 3$. Recognizing $3^x$ as a variable lets you combine like terms.

questions.question: Solve $2^{x} = 3^{x-1}$. Give the exact answer.
questions.explanation: Take log: $x\log 2 = (x-1)\log 3$. Expand: $x\log 2 = x\log 3 - \log 3$. Isolate $x$: $x(\log 2 - \log 3) = -\log 3$ тЖТ $x = \frac{\log 3}{\log 3 - \log 2} = \frac{\log 3}{\log(3/2)}$.

questions.question: A substance decays according to $A = A_0(0.5)^{t/8}$. How long until only 10% remains? Use $\log 0.1 \approx -1$ and $\log 0.5 \approx -0.3010$.
A population grows according to $P(t) = P_0 e^{0.03t}$. If $P(10) = 5000$, find $P_0$. Use $e^{0.3} \approx 1.3499$.
questions.explanation: $5000 = P_0 e^{0.03 \times 10} = P_0 e^{0.3}$. $P_0 = \frac{5000}{e^{0.3}} = \frac{5000}{1.3499} \approx 3704$. This is the initial amount before 10 years of continuous growth.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered solving exponential equations: using logs when bases can't be matched, isolating the exponential first, solving natural exponential equations, recognizing when common bases work, handling exponential quadratics, and applying to half-life and growth problems.
scoreMessages.70: Strong work! Key reminders: (1) Isolate the exponential term before taking logs, (2) Use ln when the base is e, use log for other bases, (3) When bases can be made the same, equate exponents without logs, (4) For $e^{2x} - 5e^x + 6 = 0$, substitute $u = e^x$ to get a quadratic.
scoreMessages.50: Good effort. Focus on: (1) After taking the log, use the Power Property to bring the exponent down, (2) For equations with different bases, take logs of both sides and solve the resulting linear equation, (3) For exponential quadratics, substitution transforms them into familiar quadratics, (4) Always check that solutions make sense in context (positive time, positive amounts).
scoreMessages.0: Review solving exponential equations: (1) If bases can be made the same, rewrite and equate exponents, (2) If not, isolate the exponential term, take log or ln of both sides, use the Power Property to bring the exponent down, and solve, (3) For $e^{kx} = a$, take ln: $kx = \ln a$, (4) For exponential quadratics, substitute $u = b^x$.
